editableNewDialogue.text: That CW4Kids rebrand still feels half-finished. Do you think they’re doing it on purpose, like a staged rollout?

Could be. Networks sometimes do a phased rollout so affiliates, ad partners, and web teams aren’t forced to switch everything overnight. It’s also a way to test whether the new look lands before committing to a full bumper/package overhaul.
It’s weird seeing the old vibe with new shows. The slogans and bumpers feel like they’re from two different blocks.

That mismatch is common during ownership or branding transitions—graphics update first, then on-air packaging, then the website/app, and finally the program library and promos. If they’re still using legacy templates, you’ll get that “two eras at once” feeling.
GoGoRiki is actually adorable though. I didn’t expect to like it.

It’s got that rounded, storybook character design that plays well in short comedic plots—easy to schedule, easy to rerun, easy for new viewers to drop into. If the block leans more comedy-heavy, it’s the kind of show that can quietly become a centerpiece.
Do you think it could replace a lot of the current comedy cartoons if they keep pushing it?

Potentially, yes—especially if they want a consistent tone and a show that’s flexible for marathons and weekend blocks. A simple cast, episodic structure, and broad humor make it “schedule-friendly.” The main limiter is whether they have enough episodes (or can commission more) to sustain frequent rotations without burnout.

If the CW4Kids site keeps those dead show pages up much longer, I’m probably going to start archiving the whole thing myself—accounts/points are neat, but it’s still got that old Kids WB skeleton underneath.
Totally get that. Those kinds of slow-roll transitions can leave a weird “half-finished” feel, especially when the branding updates faster than the actual site structure.

Hold up—small correction for the character design notes: in the newer writeups I’ve been doing, Glitter isn’t fuschia anymore. I switched her to a sparkling gold blob, so she stands out more against Gloopy’s teal.
Good catch. So the updated look is: Gloopy stays a teal slime blob, but Glitter is now a sparkling gold blob (not fuschia). That lines up with the more recent descriptions you’ve been using.
Yeah—teal + gold reads way cleaner on screen. The old fuschia idea was early concept stuff.
